test_cases.description: Posture enum has all 5 expected members
assertions: All 5 posture members exist: adversarial, contrarian, critic, skeptic, collaborator

test_cases.description: Posture enum members serialize to their string values (StrEnum)
Constructing Posture with invalid value raises ValueError
Phase literal type accepts all 4 valid values
assertions: All 4 phase values exist: understand, challenge, synthesize, complete

test_cases.description: Status literal type accepts incomplete and complete
assertions: Both status values exist: incomplete, complete

test_cases.description: Severity literal type accepts must and should
assertions: Both severity values exist: must, should

test_cases.description: Role literal type accepts user and assistant
assertions: Both role values exist: user, assistant

test_cases.description: FailureMode struct can be constructed with required fields
assertions: description, severity, historical fields are accessible

test_cases.description: ProblemModel has empty defaults for all fields
assertions: All list fields default to empty lists

test_cases.description: ProblemModel instances have independent default lists (no shared mutable defaults)
assertions: Two ProblemModel instances have independent list objects

test_cases.description: apply_update replaces scalar (str) fields with new values
apply_update extends str list fields and deduplicates
apply_update appends FailureMode items without dedup
assertions: New FailureMode is appended to failure_modes list

test_cases.description: apply_update preserves original list item order when extending
assertions: Pre-existing items remain in original order before new items

test_cases.description: apply_update with empty dict makes no changes
apply_update silently ignores keys not in ProblemModel.model_fields
apply_update can update multiple fields at once
test_cases.input_description: update with system_description, stakeholders, and assumptions
assertions: All three fields are correctly updated

test_cases.description: apply_update raises error when scalar field update value is not a str
assertions: Error is raised for non-str scalar value

test_cases.description: apply_update raises error when list field update value is neither a list nor valid single item
assertions: Error is raised for invalid list update value

test_cases.description: apply_update raises error when failure_modes item cannot be validated as FailureMode
assertions: Error is raised for invalid FailureMode dict

test_cases.description: apply_update handles a single string for a list[str] field if supported
assertions: Either appends single item or raises clear error

test_cases.description: safe_parse with valid dict returns correctly populated LLMResponseMeta
safe_parse with empty dict returns safe defaults
safe_parse with partial dict fills defaults for missing keys
safe_parse with wrong value types returns safe defaults (never raises)
safe_parse ignores extra keys in raw dict
safe_parse with non-dict input returns safe defaults
safe_parse never raises regardless of bizarre input
assertions: No exceptions for None, int, list, nested garbage

test_cases.description: Message can be constructed with role and content, timestamp defaults to UTC now
Constraint can be constructed with all required fields
assertions: All 6 fields are accessible: id, boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale

test_cases.description: to_yaml_dict returns dict with all 6 expected keys
assertions: Dict has keys: id, boundary, condition, violation, severity, rationale

test_cases.description: to_yaml_dict serializes severity as plain string not enum object
to_yaml_dict produces all string values (YAML-safe primitives)
assertions: All values in returned dict are str instances

test_cases.description: to_yaml_dict output can be passed through yaml.safe_dump without error
assertions: yaml.safe_dump(result) succeeds and produces valid YAML string

test_cases.description: Session can be constructed with defaults, generates unique id and UTC timestamps
assertions: id is a valid UUID string

test_cases.description: Two Session instances get different default ids
Session.touch() updates updated_at to current UTC time
assertions: updated_at after touch >= updated_at before touch

test_cases.description: Session.touch() does not modify any field other than updated_at
assertions: id, current_phase, current_round, posture, etc. all unchanged

test_cases.description: Session.to_json() returns valid JSON string
Session.to_json() uses 2-space indentation
Posture is serialized as plain string value not 'Posture.xxx'
Roundtrip: from_json(to_json()) produces equivalent Session with minimal data
assertions: Deserialized session equals original in all fields

test_cases.description: Roundtrip: from_json(to_json()) with full session including history, problem model, artifacts
from_json raises error on invalid JSON string
assertions: Exception is raised for malformed JSON

test_cases.description: from_json raises error when JSON structure is missing required fields
assertions: Exception raised for missing required fields

test_cases.description: from_json raises error when posture value is not a valid Posture enum member
assertions: Exception raised for invalid posture string

test_cases.description: from_json raises error when phase is not a valid Phase literal
assertions: Exception raised for invalid phase value

test_cases.description: from_json raises error on empty string input
schema_version is preserved through serialization roundtrip
Artifacts struct holds prompt_md and constraints_yaml strings
assertions: prompt_md and constraints_yaml fields are accessible

test_cases.description: models module has no internal project imports (leaf dependency)
assertions: Module can be imported standalone without other project modules

test_cases.description: Posture is a StrEnum subclass
Core model types are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses
assertions: FailureMode, ProblemModel, Message, Constraint, Artifacts, LLMResponseMeta, Session are BaseModel subclasses
